title: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup

seo.title: Authentic Pozole Rojo Recipe: Mexican Red Hominy Soup
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Pozole Rojo with our easy recipe! This hearty Mexican red hominy soup is perfect for any occasion. Get the best Pozole Rojo recipe here.
seo.h1: The Best Authentic Pozole Rojo Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Pozole Rojo: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Pozole Rojo
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Pozole Rojo, a traditional Mexican red hominy soup garnished with fresh toppings.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Pozole Rojo, a vibrant and flavorful Mexican red hominy soup.

description: Pozole Rojo is a beloved Mexican classic, a hearty and comforting red hominy soup perfect for celebrations or a cozy family meal. This traditional dish features tender pork simmered in a rich, spicy broth made from dried chiles, creating a depth of flavor that is truly irresistible. Garnished with fresh ingredients, it's a vibrant and satisfying culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: half chopped, half thinly sliced for garnish

instructions.title: Cook the Pork
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine the pork cubes with half of the chopped onion, 2 whole garlic cloves, and enough water or broth to cover. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 45-60 minutes or until the pork is tender and easily shredded. Remove pork, shred, and set aside. Strain the cooking liquid and reserve.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chile Sauce
instructions.text: While the pork cooks, toast the dried guajillo and ancho chiles in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn them. Transfer the toasted chiles to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for 15-20 minutes until softened.

instructions.title: Blend the Chile Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the rehydrated chiles and place them in a blender with the remaining chopped onion, 2-3 garlic cloves, Mexican oregano, cumin, and about 1 cup of the reserved pork cooking liquid or fresh broth. Blend until very smooth. For an extra fine sauce, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, pressing on the solids to extract all liquid.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: In the same pot used for the pork, add a little oil if necessary. Pour in the strained chile sauce and cook over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it darkens slightly and thickens. This step deepens the flavor.

instructions.title: Simmer the Pozole
instructions.text: Add the shredded pork, drained hominy, and the remaining reserved pork cooking liquid or fresh broth to the pot with the chile sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld beautifully. Season with salt to taste.

instructions.title: Serve with Garnishes
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Pozole Rojo into bowls. Serve immediately with a generous array of fresh garnishes such as thinly sliced white onion, shredded cabbage, sliced radishes, a squeeze of fresh lime juice, and diced avocado. Enjoy with tostadas or tortilla chips on the side.

tips: Always toast your chiles dry before rehydrating them; this brings out their smoky flavor and aroma, essential for an authentic Pozole Rojo.
For the best texture, use cacahuazintle hominy if available, or ensure your canned hominy is well-rinsed. You can also adjust the spice level by adding a dried chile de arbol to the chile sauce.
Pozole tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop and add fresh garnishes before serving.
article: Pozole Rojo Red Hominy Soup is a beloved Mexican Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
